skill.name: volcano-plot-script
skill.description: Generate R/Python code for volcano plots from DEG (Differentially Expressed Genes) analysis results. Triggered when user needs visualization of gene expression data, p-value vs fold-change scatter plots, publication-ready figures for bioinformatics analysis.
skill.summary: Bioinformatics skill that generates customizable R or Python volcano plots from differential gene expression data for publication-ready figures.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 53 static analysis findings were evaluated. 52 of 53 are false positives triggered by pattern matching on markdown documentation (bash code blocks, inline file references, bioinformatics package names). One true positive (Python file write at scripts/main.py:295) is a legitimate feature for exporting generated R scripts. No evidence of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or command injection. The skill is a standard bioinformatics data visualization tool.

low_findings.title: File Write for R Script Export
low_findings.description: Python open() call writes a generated R script to the filesystem. This is a legitimate feature of the skill for exporting R scripts alongside the generated plot. The output path is derived from the user-specified --output argument with a .R extension.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is an intentional feature for R script export. The output path is controlled by user argument, not hardcoded. Low risk as the skill already handles user-provided file paths throughout.

content.user_title: Generate Volcano Plots for Gene Expression Data
content.value_statement: Create publication-ready volcano plots from differential gene expression results without writing plotting code from scratch. This skill generates Python or R scripts that produce high-quality figures from standard DEG analysis data.
actual_capabilities: Generate volcano plots from DEG analysis results in CSV or TSV format
Classify genes as upregulated, downregulated, or not significant based on configurable thresholds
Customize log2 fold change threshold, p-value threshold, colors, and figure dimensions
Label genes of interest from a provided gene list or by top N most significant genes
Export plots as PNG, PDF, or SVG at configurable DPI (default 300)
Generate equivalent R script for use in ggplot2-based workflows
limitations: Requires pre-computed DEG analysis results as input - does not perform differential expression analysis
Best suited for single comparison volcano plots, not multi-panel or faceted layouts
Gene labels may overlap on dense plots; manual adjustment may be needed for publication
Plotting performance may decrease with datasets exceeding 50,000 genes

use_cases.title: Visualize RNA-seq DEG Analysis Results
use_cases.description: Input DESeq2 or edgeR result files and generate volcano plots showing the distribution of differentially expressed genes with clear significance thresholds.

use_cases.title: Generate Publication-Ready Figures
use_cases.description: Create high-resolution volcano plots with custom colors, axis labels, and gene annotations for use in scientific manuscripts and presentations.
use_cases.target_user: Academic researchers preparing figures for papers or conferences

use_cases.title: Identify and Label Key Marker Genes
use_cases.description: Upload a list of known marker genes alongside DEG results to produce a volcano plot with specific genes highlighted and labeled for quick visual assessment.
use_cases.target_user: Computational biologists exploring gene expression patterns

prompt_templates.title: Basic Volcano Plot Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a volcano plot from my DEG results file at path/to/results.csv. Use default thresholds for significance.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick visualization of gene expression data with standard settings

prompt_templates.title: Custom Threshold Volcano Plot
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a volcano plot from my DEG data at path/to/data.csv. Set log2 fold change threshold to 0.58 and p-value threshold to 0.01. Use blue for upregulated and green for downregulated genes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Customized analysis with user-specified significance cutoffs and colors

prompt_templates.title: Volcano Plot with Gene Labels
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a volcano plot from the file results/deg_analysis.csv. Label the top 20 most significant genes and also highlight genes from the list in references/markers.txt.
prompt_templates.scenario: Identifying and labeling key genes of interest on the plot

prompt_templates.title: Publication Figure with R Script Export
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a publication-ready volcano plot from my DESeq2 results at data/deseq2_results.csv. Use log2FC threshold of 1.0 and padj threshold of 0.05. Export at 600 DPI as PDF. Also export an equivalent R script and save the list of significant genes to sig_genes.csv.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating a complete publication-ready output package with multiple file formats

output_examples.input: CSV file with columns: gene, log2FoldChange, padj. Contains 15,000 gene expression records from a DESeq2 analysis comparing treated vs control samples.
output_examples.output: A volcano plot image showing three groups of points: upregulated genes in red (upper right), downregulated genes in blue (upper left), and non-significant genes in gray (center). Dashed threshold lines mark the log2FC and p-value cutoffs. A summary box shows count statistics.

output_examples.input: DEG results file plus a text file listing 25 known biomarker genes. Request to label the top 10 most significant genes.
output_examples.output: A volcano plot with the top 10 significant genes annotated by name near their data points. The 25 biomarker genes are highlighted with distinct markers. Legend shows upregulated, downregulated, and non-significant categories with counts.

best_practices: Ensure input data contains the required columns: log2 fold change, adjusted p-value, and gene identifiers before generating the plot
Use FDR-corrected p-values (padj) instead of raw p-values for more reliable significance calls in differential expression analysis
Select colorblind-friendly color palettes to ensure accessibility of published figures for all readers
anti_patterns: Labeling too many genes on a single plot, which creates visual clutter and makes labels unreadable; limit to top 10-20 genes
Using raw p-values instead of adjusted p-values, which inflates false positive rates due to multiple testing
Applying arbitrary thresholds without biological context; fold change and p-value thresholds should reflect the experimental design and sample size

faq.question: What input file format does the skill require?
faq.answer: The skill accepts CSV or TSV files containing differential expression results. Required columns are log2 fold change, adjusted p-value, and gene identifiers.

faq.question: What output formats are supported for the plot?
faq.answer: The generated volcano plot can be saved as PNG, PDF, or SVG. The default output format is PNG at 300 DPI, suitable for publication.

faq.question: Can I customize the appearance of the volcano plot?
faq.answer: Yes. You can set custom colors for upregulated, downregulated, and non-significant genes. You can also adjust figure size, DPI, axis labels, and the plot title.

faq.question: Does the skill work with R in addition to Python?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --export-r flag to generate an equivalent R script using ggplot2. This allows you to further customize the plot in an R environment.

faq.question: How are genes classified as upregulated or downregulated?
faq.answer: Genes are classified based on two thresholds: log2 fold change threshold (default 1.0) and p-value threshold (default 0.05). Genes above both thresholds are upregulated, below both are downregulated, and all others are non-significant.

faq.question: Can I highlight specific genes on the plot?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide a text file with gene names to label, or use the --top-n parameter to automatically label the N most significant genes by p-value.
